The sample does not create any new processes, but it loads several notable modules, including imm32.dll, which is often used for keyboard hooking. The highlighted API call to SetWindowsHookExA also su

The sample does not appear to make any registry changes for persistence. This suggests that the sample may not be using traditional persistence methods. The next step is to investigate the network act

The sample does not appear to have any network activity, including DNS lookups or HTTP conversations. This suggests that the sample may be operating in a sandboxed environment or may not be communicat

The sample drops a single file, C0ATO.exe, in the user's downloads directory. This file is the same as the original sample, suggesting that the sample may be attempting to persist itself on the system
